name: Physical Security
description: Physical access controls for OT environments. Physical security is the foundation of OT security — all network controls can be bypassed if an attacker has physical access to a PLC cabinet or engineering workstation. Field sites (remote RTUs, unmanned substations, pipeline valve stations) are particularly vulnerable.

requirements.name: Physical access control to OT assets
requirements.description: OT systems must be physically secured with access controls appropriate to the criticality of the assets. SIS controllers require the highest level of physical security.

technical.summary: Layered physical security: facility perimeter, OT area access, control room access, individual cabinet access. Each layer adds friction for unauthorised access.
actions: Control room: card access control (individual access cards — not a door code shared by all)
PLC/DCS cabinet rooms: locked cabinets, limited key access
SIS cabinet room: additional access control beyond DCS room; access log mandatory
Field sites (RTUs, unmanned substations): perimeter fencing, cabinet locks, tamper detection
Visitor escort policy in all OT areas
CCTV in control rooms, cabinet rooms, and facility entry points
Physical access log for SIS area maintained manually

governance.summary: Physical access rights reviewed quarterly. Access removed immediately when personnel leave or change role.

slMapping.sl2: Card access control; visitor escort; CCTV
slMapping.sl3: Perimeter + room + cabinet layered access; access logs; automatic alert on after-hours access
slMapping.sl4: SIS room requires dual-person access; physical tamper detection on SIS cabinets; biometric option

requirements.name: Field site physical security
requirements.description: Remote OT field sites (substations, pump stations, RTU sites, pipeline valve stations) are typically unmanned and present unique physical security challenges. These sites are often targeted for initial access to larger OT networks.

technical.summary: Remote sites require physical tamper detection, secure enclosures, and detection of unauthorised entry linked to central monitoring.
actions: Perimeter fencing for remote sites where feasible
Tamper-evident seals or tamper detection sensors on RTU/field device cabinets
Camera/motion detection at unmanned sites with alert to control room or security centre
Cellular modem cabinets locked separately from process control equipment
Physical inspection of remote sites on scheduled rotation (frequency based on criticality)
Intrusion alarm linked to SCADA operator workstation or security monitoring centre

governance.summary: Field site physical security standard with inspection schedule. Tamper events escalated to OT security team.
actions: Regular site inspection schedule based on criticality
Annual physical security audit of remote sites

slMapping.sl3: Tamper detection; camera/alarm; inspection schedule; tamper event alerting
slMapping.sl4: Continuous tamper monitoring with immediate alert; armed response for highest-criticality remote sites
